Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 101. Inspecting and Counting Logs (ABL 0397)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL EN-ia

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Ṭab-ṣill-Ešarra. Ešarra is well, the temples are well, the Inner City is well, Assyria is well, may the king my lord be well! May Aššur and Mullissu bless the king, my lord!

o 22

ARAD-ka mDÙG.GA—ṣil—É.ŠÁR.RA

o 33

DI-mu a-na É.ŠÁR.RA.KI

o 44

DI-mu a-na É.KUR-MEŠ

o 55

DI-mu a-na URU.ŠÀ—URU

o 66

DI-mu a-na KUR—aš-šur.KI

o 77

lu DI-mu a-na LUGAL EN-ia

o 88

aš-šur dNIN.LÍL a-na LUGAL EN-ia

o 99

lik-ru-bu ina UGU GIŠ.ÙR-MEŠ

(9) As to the logs which the king wr[ote me about ......]

o 1010

ša LUGAL ⸢EN*⸣ [iš-pur-an-ni]

rest broken away

(Break)

Reverse
rr beginning broken away

r 1'1'

[x x x x x x x x] ⸢x x⸣ [x]+⸢x⸣

(r 1) [..... are] very [...], im[possible] to count. There are many logs! The king, my lord, can be glad.

r 2'2'

[x x x x x x x x] a—dan-niš

r 3'3'

⸢la*⸣ [il-lak a]-na* ma-né-e

r 4'4'

ma-a-du GIŠ.ÙR-MEŠ

r 5'5'

lib-bu ša LUGAL EN-ia

r 6'6'

lu-u DÙG.GA

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334272/.
